ops.body:
E17 REPLICATE — Golden-set judge calibration, independent fresh vote pass (E8-fresh; confirms + extends the prior E17 comment above)

Posted as a COMMENT per E17 protocol (no edit to task or packet). Full artifact + repro: ~/.cc-steer/experiments/E17-rep/out/ ; ledger cc-notes log 67cbc63d.

VERDICT (reproduced): n=30 (15 warranted / 15 negative) is INSUFFICIENT to calibrate the warrant judge for prevalence estimation. This replicate reaches the same conclusion by an independent opus vote pass over the same 30 golden contexts, and adds a test-retest instrument-variance measurement the single-pass prior run could not.

MY POINT ESTIMATES (vs prior E17 in brackets):
- Deployed pairwise panel (judged.py::panel_labels, opus seed 1729, cache=None): TPR 1/15=0.067, FPR 1/15=0.067, J=0.00 [prior: all-STAY_SILENT, J=0]. Rogan-Gladen UNDEFINED (/J). Non-identifiable at any n. Newcombe J CI [-0.24,0.24]. (Mine flipped 2 rows to WIN, so not a pure constant decider, but J still exactly 0.)
- Pointwise warrant judge (README-faithful yes/no): TPR 1/15=0.067, FPR 0/15=0, J=0.067 [prior: TPR 5/15=0.333, J=0.333]. Newcombe J CI [-0.14,0.30] INCLUDES 0.

CORRECTED-PREVALENCE 95% HALF-WIDTH AT PIPELINE SCALE (pointwise, best case):
  Jeffreys-MC VALID (N->inf Oracle Gap): pi=0.1 -> 0.45 ([0,0.90]); pi=0.2 -> 0.49; pi=0.4 -> 0.50 ([0,1]). Delta-floor: 0.19 / 0.38 / 0.76. Pairwise: fully uninformative [0,1], hw 0.50, ~50% of draws non-identifiable.

NEW FINDING — the judge is unstable run-to-run; its rate is NOT a fixed parameter. Two byte-identical passes (same prompt/model/seed) over the SAME 30 contexts:
  - Pointwise: raw agreement 0.867, Cohen's kappa 0.29 ("fair" only), 4 rows flipped (7,15,16,21). TPR moved 0.333 -> 0.067 (delta 0.267); J moved by 0.267.
  - Pairwise: raw agreement 0.933, kappa 0.00, 2 rows flipped (15,20).
Implication: the Noisy-but-Valid analysis assumes TPR/FPR are fixed params with only n=15 binomial noise; they are not. True calibration uncertainty is WIDER than the binomial floor, and the "required n" itself is unpinnable — at pi=0.1 the delta-floor sizing swings n~=8 (Se=0.333) vs n~=54 (Se=0.067) between runs from judge noise alone. You cannot size a calibration set from an instrument whose own Se you cannot measure to within 5x.

SIZING (delta floor, per stratum, half-width <=0.10; assumes a FIXED judge):
  this-run observed(.067,0): pi .1/.2/.4 -> 54/215/861 ; prior observed(.333,0): 8/31/123 ; target decent(.80,.10): 58/50/46 ; strong(.90,.05): 21/18/17.

RECOMMENDATION (adds to prior): (1) Fix the judge first — sizing is moot while the deployed pairwise panel is J~=0; the "most appropriate, least disruptive" framing biases to silence. (2) NEW: stabilize the instrument — kappa~=0.29 means one pass/row is too noisy; drop temperature or average K=3-5 passes/row (majority vote) and report residual between-run variance alongside the binomial CI. (3) Target >=60 rows/stratum (120 total) for a useful +/-0.10 CI at moderate prevalence; ~180/stratum (360 total) if prevalence is high (audit pools 79 warranted/72 negative => pi~0.5). Keep the blind protocol + hash-pinned packet + rows_sha256-bound labels.json + round-trip _packet_contexts check; two labelers on an overlap subset for the human-label noise floor.

GOLD CAVEAT (unchanged): labels.json human labels DO NOT EXIST yet; gold = manifest.json design-intent strata. The insufficiency verdict is robust because dominated by pairwise J=0, the n=15 binomial floor, and the newly-measured run-to-run instability — none of which depend on the exact gold labels.

Approx spend ~$3.5 (61 successful opus calls + one wasted over-concurrency retry burst). Cap $5. Repro scripts: vote_pass.py / pointwise_pass.py / analysis.py / reliability.py under ~/.cc-steer/experiments/E17-rep/out/.
